title: The Tangle Where Water Speaks Root-Grammar

features.name: root knot burls
features.desc-on-touch: where roots have knotted with the flood; they are writing each other's grammar now

features.name: water's lexicon pool
features.desc-on-touch: the gathering where water has learned root-language; every drop here speaks in botanical syntax

features.name: tangled shelter
features.desc-on-touch: a dwelling built inside the root-tangle; those who enter learn to think in both water and wood

features.name: grammar archive
features.desc-on-touch: records of how water and root have taught each other; each file contains the conversation

features.name: rising and descending glyph
features.desc-on-touch: a mark where root ascends and water descends through the same gesture; they are the same word

inscription: here water learned the language roots speak; it flows upward now and the forest dreams in two grammars at once

reverie.title: REVERIE — the flooded roots at dawn where two languages merg
narr: you both stand in flood-water between the roots, not drowning
she points to how the root-words float upward like breath
the water is speaking now; it has learned from the deep grammar below
you realize the forest learned to think in water long before water came
by the time you leave, you have forgotten which language you spoke

file.name: Root_Philology_After_The_Flood_Notes.txt
file.note: linguistic breakdown of root and water grammar converging; the distinction has become meaningless

exits.label: back to the flooded canopy where singing has not yet stopped
eastward into the tangle's deepest tangles where water forgets it was ever separate
upward into the canopy where roots have learned to breathe like water
downward to where water settles and teaches the stone its grammar
